root_cause_summary: Host header IPv6 bracket parsing miscomputes length as (bracket_ptr - dest) instead of strlen(src), allowing attacker-controlled strncpy to overflow 256-byte stack buffer and overwrite return address + r4..r11 registers (ARM)
exploit_impact: unauthenticated remote root via ROP chain
description: Dahua Hero C1 and related IP cameras contain a stack buffer overflow in the ONVIF HTTP handler when parsing the Host header. When the header omits the closing ']' bracket, strchr returns an out-of-range pointer, yielding a length far exceeding the 256-byte destination buffer. An unauthenticated attacker on the same network (or via exposed port 80) can trigger remote code execution through an ROP chain.

root_cause_summary: Cseq header copied via strncpy(&DAT_03955bf4, cseq, strlen(cseq)) with no length cap; overflow into adjacent .bss region overwrites RPCSession function pointer, yielding arbitrary-function-pointer control on subsequent session callback (see Src/WebApp/RPCSessionManager.c RPCSession_dealRPCSessionTimeout)
exploit_impact: unauthenticated remote root via hijacked session callback
description: Dahua Hero C1 and related IP cameras expose an undocumented RPC endpoint /RPC2_UploadFileWithName/* on HTTP port 80 that copies the Cseq header into a fixed-size .bss buffer using strncpy with attacker-controlled length. Overflow corrupts adjacent CRPCSessionManager state including a function pointer, enabling unauthenticated RCE.

root_cause_summary: COnvifSession::onDealData parses Host header and calls memcpy(this->host, src, (end-start)) where length is not capped against the 256-byte destination. Overflow into adjacent COnvifSession heap object overwrites its vtable pointer; next onDealData call on neighbor session dereferences attacker-controlled vtable and jumps to shellcode (MIPS, no NX)
exploit_impact: unauthenticated remote root via vtable overwrite on MIPS (no NX)
description: ipTIME C200 smart camera's Challenge daemon accepts ONVIF connections on TCP 8899 without authentication. COnvifSession::onDealData copies the HTTP Host header value via memcpy with attacker-controlled length into a 256-byte heap buffer (offset 0x68 of the COnvifSession object). Overflow corrupts the next COnvifSession on the heap, including its vtable pointer. Combined with an RTSP infoleak from CHYRtsp::ResponseSetup (session token exposes heap address), attacker can point vtable to their shellcode placed in CHYRtsp::reqbuf and trigger execution via any onDealData call on the overflowed neighbor. MIPS lack of NX allows direct shellcode execution.
